items.title: IRS reminds taxpayers: request a filing extension by April 15 to file until Oct. 15
items.summary: The IRS said taxpayers can get an automatic extension to file their 2025 federal return until Oct. 15, 2026, but the extension request must be made by April 15, 2026. The IRS emphasized the extension is for filing paperwork only—tax payments are still due April 15 to avoid penalties and interest, and some groups (e.g., taxpayers abroad and some military) have automatic filing extensions to June 15.

items.title: CMS guidance: new limits on claiming federal Medicaid/CHIP funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued guidance to states on implementing a statutory change that limits when states can claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or nationals (or are in certain noncitizen categories identified in law), effective Oct. 1, 2026. CMS noted exceptions including continued federal matching for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the change does not affect the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: HHS/ACF proposes reallotment of FY 2025 LIHEAP funds; public comments due Apr. 30
items.summary: HHS's Administration for Children and Families (Office of Community Services) published a Federal Register notice proposing reallotment of remaining FY 2025 LIHEAP funds to eligible grantees. The notice states the comment period ends April 30, 2026.

items.title: HUD rescinds prior streamlined waiver notice for faster admissions into HCV/PBV/Public Housing for people experiencing homelessness
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-04 rescinds Notice PIH 2024-17, which had described a streamlined review process for regulatory waivers to expedite admissions into the Housing Choice Voucher (HCV), Project-Based Voucher (PBV), and Public Housing programs for people experiencing homelessness. HUD said waivers already approved under the rescinded process remain valid until their expiration under the terms of approval.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: Maryland Eastern Shore Housing Choice Voucher waiting list opens Apr. 1–Apr. 30 (online only)
items.summary: Maryland DHCD announced its Eastern Shore Housing Choice Voucher waiting list will accept online applications from 9:00 a.m. April 1, 2026 through 5:00 p.m. April 30, 2026. DHCD said applications submitted during the open period will have an equal chance through a computerized random selection process.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash benefit; states must allow at least one non-fresh form by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS set FY 2026 WIC monthly cash-value benefit amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. FNS also said recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: Social Security: 2026 COLA is 2.8%; SSI federal payment standard rises to $994/month for individuals
items.summary: SSA reported a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 for Social Security beneficiaries and SSI recipients. SSA's fact sheet lists the SSI federal payment standard increasing to $994/month for an individual and $1,491/month for a couple, and the maximum taxable earnings for Social Security increasing to $184,500.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: maximum Pell Grant for 2026–27 is $7,395
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education's Federal Student Aid said the maximum Federal Pell Grant award for the 2026–27 award year is $7,395. The agency emphasized students must submit the FAFSA each year to be considered for Pell Grants.
